description: Systematic audit of every place the dome model borrows from WGS84, GPS, or globe-derived infrastructure without acknowledgment. Complements gravity-dependency-map.json (NOTE-006). Together they show the dome model is a thin wrapper around globe infrastructure, not an independent cosmological framework.
methodology: For each WIN, classify the WGS84/globe dependency level and trace the specific dependency chain: what globe-derived quantity flows in, through what formula or assumption, to produce what dome 'prediction.' Sources: dome repo code (john09289/predictions), dome raw text (raw-text/), wins.json code_analysis tags, globe fingerprint tracker results, gravity dependency map.

summary.independent_note: These 6 WINs are either purely theological (031, 032, 034), a literature citation with no computation (015), or qualitative claims with no numerical content (003, 033). None produces a testable dome-derived prediction.
summary.conclusion: If you removed all WGS84/globe-derived inputs from the dome model, 61 of 67 WINs would have no computable prediction. The remaining 6 are unfalsifiable or computational vacuums. The dome model is not an alternative cosmology — it is a display layer over globe infrastructure.

dependency_categories.COORD: WGS84 latitude/longitude used as input coordinates (cannot compute dome position without globe coordinates — OPEN-001)
dependency_categories.CONST: Globe-derived physical constant hardcoded (Earth radius, obliquity, gravity, orbital parameters)
dependency_categories.DATA: Observation data processed through globe-based models (IGRF spherical harmonics, CHAOS-7, Besselian eclipse elements, JPL ephemerides, tidal models)
dependency_categories.CALIB: Instruments calibrated against globe infrastructure (gravimeters, INTERMAGNET baselines, GPS timing, satellite orbits)
dependency_categories.FORMULA: Mathematical formula that is spherical geometry in disguise (Schumann eigenvalues, tidal harmonics, Coriolis, spherical harmonic decomposition)
dependency_categories.FITTED: Dome parameter fitted to globe-measured distances/observations (disc_radius, e=0.66, 0.9941 scale, FSF values)

OPEN-001.impact: Every WIN that references a geographic location depends on WGS84 — the dome cannot place a single station, city, or measurement site without globe coordinates
OPEN-001.affected_wins: All 67 WINs that reference specific locations (effectively all except theological WINs)

OPEN-003.text: Lunar orbital mechanics (no dome lunar theory)
OPEN-003.impact: All tidal WINs (045-051), eclipse WINs (010-014, 025), lunar magnetic WIN (039), 18.6-yr cycle (020) have no dome-derived timing or amplitude

OPEN-006.text: WGS84 latitude bias at high latitudes
OPEN-006.impact: Dome acknowledges its coordinate system deviates from reality — plans inclinometer tests to measure the bias, using globe coordinates as baseline

OPEN-007.impact: Dome cannot explain how GPS satellites (requiring Keplerian orbits at 20,200 km + relativistic corrections) produce imagery consistent with globe geometry

OPEN-011.impact: The two-pole model (WIN-053) is claimed as confirmed while OPEN-011 admits it doesn't work. Internal contradiction.

OPEN-015.text: Equatorial coordinate failure — Singapore and similar near-equatorial cities 'fall beyond the equatorial ring'
OPEN-015.impact: ~40% of world population lives in the tropics below ±12° latitude. Dome coordinate system fails for them entirely.

disc_radius_20015.globe_equivalent: π × R_Earth = π × 6,371 km = 20,015 km (polar semi-circumference)
disc_radius_20015.why_globe: On a flat disc, the pole-to-rim distance is a straight radial line. There is no geometric reason a flat disc's radius should involve π. π arises from the spherical surface integral (arc length on a sphere), not from linear distance on a plane.

gravity_9780.globe_equivalent: WGS84 Somigliana normal gravity formula g₀ at equator
gravity_9780.source_file: monitor.py line 465, labeled 'source: WGS84 standard' at line 474
gravity_9780.why_globe: This is the WGS84 reference gravity value. The dome has no gravitational mechanism to derive any value of g.

obliquity_23_44.dome_value: 23.44° / 23.45° in coordinate conversion and solar formula
obliquity_23_44.globe_equivalent: Earth's axial obliquity (tilt of rotation axis relative to orbital plane)
obliquity_23_44.why_globe: Obliquity is the angle between equatorial and ecliptic planes — concepts that require a spherical rotating body orbiting the Sun. A flat disc has no axial tilt, no obliquity, no ecliptic.

sun_altitude_5733.globe_equivalent: 0.9 × R_Earth (6,371 km), fitted from crepuscular ray elevation angles measured on globe
sun_altitude_5733.why_globe: Exceeds dome's own firmament height H(r) at every solar position (1.58× at Tropic of Cancer, 2.14× at equator). update_optical_caveats.py explicitly suppresses this self-contradiction by relabeling altitude as 'optical illusion'.

firmament_8537.globe_equivalent: No clean globe match, but used as parameter in H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619)
firmament_8537.why_globe: While H₀ itself doesn't match a globe constant, the entire H(r) function is fitted to reproduce globe-consistent observations (Schumann resonance, stellar observations, aircraft distances) that arise from spherical geometry.

moon_altitude_2534.globe_equivalent: No direct match, but eclipse timing and shadow geometry borrowed from Besselian elements computed for distant moon at 384,400 km
moon_altitude_2534.why_globe: At 2,534 km, local moon produces single tidal spike (gravitational 1/r² falloff gives sharp peak under nearest point). Observed semidiurnal pattern (two equal bulges 180° apart) requires distant moon where tidal force gradient is nearly uniform across Earth's diameter.

earth_diameter_12717.why_globe: This parameter corresponds to no dome structural dimension. It is Earth's diameter, hidden inside the Tesla resonance formula.

eccentricity_0_66.globe_equivalent: Fitted to minimize EW distance errors against WGS84 geodesic distances
eccentricity_0_66.why_globe: E(0.4356) computation contains a mathematical error (dome says 1.211, actual is 1.3824). No V13 Finsler code exists in dome repo. The entire parameter is a curve-fit artifact against globe measurements.

ew_scale_0_9941.globe_equivalent: Least-squares fit against 6 WGS84-derived route distances
ew_scale_0_9941.why_globe: No physical derivation, no independent confirmation. Dome's own AI context page lists 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' as an open question. Reverse-engineered from WGS84 data, then claimed as prediction.

km_per_degree_111.dome_value: 111.0 km/deg in monitor.py drift rate computation
km_per_degree_111.why_globe: This conversion factor assumes each degree of latitude spans a fixed distance — true on a sphere. On a flat AE disc, km/deg varies with radius.

kappa_1_67.globe_equivalent: Empirical ratio from globe-timed eclipse observations (10.9 nT / 6.5 μGal)
kappa_1_67.why_globe: Both numerator and denominator are measurements taken during globe-predicted eclipses at WGS84-positioned stations. κ is doubly parasitic on globe infrastructure.

hubble_constant_70.dome_value: λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc (dome calls it 'aetheric wavelength')
hubble_constant_70.globe_equivalent: H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc, the Hubble constant from globe-based Cepheid distance ladder
hubble_constant_70.why_globe: z = D/λ_A is algebraically identical to Hubble's law z = H₀d/c. No dome parameter appears in the formula. H₀ is borrowed from standard cosmology without modification.

sidereal_day_23_9345.globe_equivalent: Earth's sidereal rotation period, differs from solar day by 1/T_year (orbital motion)
sidereal_day_23_9345.why_globe: The sidereal/solar day difference encodes Earth's orbital motion around the Sun. On a flat disc with circling local sun, this relationship doesn't hold.

IGRF_spherical_harmonics.what: International Geomagnetic Reference Field — spherical harmonic expansion of Earth's magnetic field using Gauss coefficients g_n^m, h_n^m on a sphere
IGRF_spherical_harmonics.dome_usage: All magnetic pole positions, SAA definitions, field decay rates
IGRF_spherical_harmonics.why_globe: IGRF uses Legendre polynomial basis functions defined on a sphere. The dome's radially symmetric B(r) field cannot reproduce localized features (SAA, pole positions) that IGRF captures via non-axisymmetric (m≠0) harmonics.

CHAOS7_model.what: Comprehensive magnetic field model using spherical harmonics, reference radius 6371.2 km, satellite orbit inputs
CHAOS7_model.dome_usage: SAA cell positions, decay rates, formation dates, field strength values
CHAOS7_model.why_globe: CHAOS-7 explicitly uses globe radius as reference. All SAA metrics (positions, strengths, trends) are CHAOS-7 outputs interpreted on a sphere.

besselian_eclipse_elements.what: Standard astronomical technique projecting Moon's shadow cone onto spherical Earth using JPL DE440/441 ephemerides
besselian_eclipse_elements.dome_usage: Eclipse timing, shadow paths, station coverage percentages for all eclipse WINs
besselian_eclipse_elements.why_globe: Requires spherical Earth (R≈6371 km), distant Moon (~384,400 km), geocentric coordinates. On flat disc with local moon at 2,534 km, shadow geometry would be fundamentally different.

INTERMAGNET_network.what: Global network of magnetic observatories, baselines corrected using IGRF/CHAOS models, stations positioned via WGS84
INTERMAGNET_network.dome_usage: All magnetic field measurements cited as evidence
INTERMAGNET_network.why_globe: Station baseline corrections use spherical harmonic models. Position coordinates are WGS84. Absolute calibrations assume globe-derived ambient field values.

globe_tidal_models.what: Newtonian luni-solar tidal predictions using JPL ephemerides, spherical Earth, ocean loading on spherical mesh
globe_tidal_models.dome_usage: SG gravimeter residuals are computed AFTER subtracting globe tidal predictions
globe_tidal_models.why_globe: A zero residual (WIN-013, WIN-014) means globe tidal model predicted gravity correctly to nanogal precision. If dome's gravity model were correct, residuals would be non-zero.

WGS84_gravity_formula.what: Somigliana normal gravity formula: g(φ) = g_e(1 + k·sin²φ)/√(1 - e²·sin²φ)
WGS84_gravity_formula.dome_usage: monitor.py line 465-474 uses exact WGS84 g_e = 9.7803 m/s², explicitly labeled 'source: WGS84 standard'
WGS84_gravity_formula.why_globe: The dome literally imports the globe's gravity formula and labels it as its own.

globe_distance_measurements.what: Intercity distances measured via WGS84 geodesics (Vincenty formula on ellipsoid)
globe_distance_measurements.dome_usage: All distance verification in coordinate system (WIN-027, 030, 057), explicitly labeled 'great circle' in distance_analysis.py
globe_distance_measurements.why_globe: Dome parameters (a, e, 0.9941) are fitted to minimize error against these globe distances. The optimization target IS globe geometry.

Doodson_tidal_decomposition.what: Harmonic decomposition of tidal forcing using lunar/solar orbital elements in spherical geometry
Doodson_tidal_decomposition.dome_usage: M2, S2, K1, O1, N2 periods claimed as dome predictions
Doodson_tidal_decomposition.why_globe: Each Doodson number encodes orbital angular speeds derived from Keplerian mechanics. O1's σ = τ - s uses lunar sidereal angular speed, N2's σ includes apsidal precession rate — both require elliptical orbits under gravitational perturbation.

per_win_classification.chain: disc_thickness=12,717 km = Earth diameter. f=c/(2D) is spherical waveguide formula with π cancelled. Tesla velocity πc/2.
a=πR_Earth. Eigenfrequencies follow √(n(n+1)) = spherical harmonic eigenvalues, not Bessel zeros (flat disc).
No WGS84 dependency. Purely numerological (117 Hz = 10 × Tesla freq). No dome or globe computation.
CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic source. 50.57° separation measured in globe longitude. Exponential vortex decay requires sphere-specific n(n+1) time constant.
CHAOS-7 source. Decay differential requires non-axisymmetric (m≠0) spherical harmonics. CMB-depth (r=3480 km) reversed-flux patches explain it on globe.
IGRF/gufm1 spherical harmonic pole positions. Pre-1900 data entirely globe-model reconstruction. 'North Magnetic Pole' defined via spherical dip angle.
Same IGRF dependency as WIN-006. 120°E meridian and 19.3° deviation are globe coordinates. monitor.py uses 111 km/deg (globe).
Same as WIN-001: disc_thickness = Earth diameter. 11.7 Hz cutoff IS the Schumann inter-harmonic minimum determined by spherical eigenfrequencies.
Same as WIN-008/001. Triple-counting of single formula f=c/(2D) applied to one spectral region.
Besselian eclipse elements (spherical shadow projection). INTERMAGNET stations at WGS84 positions. Coverage fractions require globe geometry.
Eclipse timing from Besselian elements. Gravimeter calibrated against WGS84 g. κ numerator from WIN-010.
κ=1.67 hardcoded from globe-timed eclipse measurements. B(r) coefficients exist only as text. monitor.py identity check: kappa_obs=10.9/6.5 (both hardcoded).
SG residual = 0.0 μGal AFTER globe tidal subtraction (Newtonian + JPL ephemerides + ocean loading on sphere). Zero residual = globe model predicted perfectly.
Same as WIN-013 across 8 Chinese SG stations. Globe tidal model baseline assumed. Besselian elements for eclipse timing.
No WGS84 dependency. Pure literature citation (Meyl scalar waves). No computation anywhere in dome code.
20.5 arcsec = v_orbital/c (globe orbital velocity). Annual period = orbital period. n(r) alpha=2.56e-8 reverse-engineered. Achromaticity violates Kramers-Kronig for any refractive medium.
Parallax measurements by Gaia/Hipparcos use astrometric solutions in heliocentric frame. 20m firmament wobble reverse-fitted to match globe observations.
6.9 min asymmetry derives from tan²(23.44°/2) — the globe's obliquity. No flat-disc mechanism for day-length asymmetry.
Analemma figure-8 requires obliquity (23.44°) and orbital eccentricity (0.0167). Dome's 8/3 ratio produces wrong shape (8-cusped epicycloid, not 2-lobed).
18.613-yr nodal period from three-body gravitational perturbation theory (Clairaut 1749). Amplitude ±5.145° = lunar orbital inclination to ecliptic — concept nonexistent on flat disc.
25,772-yr precession requires luni-solar gravitational torques on oblate spheroid. 4.87e-12 matches ω_prec × (sun_alt/H_firm). No dome torque mechanism.
monitor.py uses 111 km/deg (globe) and cos(lat) correction (spherical parallel circumference). IGRF pole positions.
~950 AD from SHAWQ2k spherical harmonic reconstruction. Dome's axisymmetric B(r) cannot produce localized SAA.
Roaring 40s exist because f=2Ω sin(φ) on rotating sphere — latitude-dependent Coriolis. On flat disc, f=2Ω is constant (no latitude bands). 48.5°S is WGS84 coordinate.
Besselian eclipse elements. Coverage fractions from globe shadow geometry. INTERMAGNET data. FSF = tan(lat)/tan(ref) — tautological identity.
5,733 km fitted from crepuscular ray angles measured on globe. Exceeds firmament at all positions. update_optical_caveats.py suppresses contradiction.
a=πR_Earth, e=0.66 fitted to globe geodesics, 0.9941 reverse-engineered from WGS84. Quadratic correction IS AE projection distortion. v44_pipeline.py does not exist (404).
Agonic line positions from IGRF spherical harmonic expansion. 180° spacing from dominant axial dipole term. Actual spacing is 208° (15.6% off claimed value).
Dome's own formulas.json (FORM-002) contains spherical eigenfrequency formula f_n=c√(n(n+1))/(2πR). Flat-disc eigenfrequency would be ~4.4 Hz, not 7.83 Hz.
distance_analysis.py optimizes ellipse parameters against 'great circle' distances (explicitly labeled). a=πR_Earth. H(r)=8537√(1−(r/20015)²) is semicircle at πR_Earth boundary.
No WGS84 dependency. Purely theological (Bible references). r=0 = North Pole is AE projection convention.
No WGS84 dependency. Purely theological. No numerical content.
No WGS84 dependency. Qualitative claim about stellar dimness. No computation, no formula, no n(r)-to-photometry link anywhere in dome code.
No WGS84 dependency. Purely theological/linguistic (biblical Hebrew etymology). No numerical values.
21,795 nT threshold from CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic model. Exists NOWHERE in dome source code. Not monitored.
IGRF-derived pole position (139.298°E from WMM2025 spherical harmonics). 120°E reference is globe longitude. Logically entailed by WIN-007 (139.3 - 120 = 19.3 > 18).
28 nT/yr from CHAOS-7/IGRF via INTERMAGNET. monitor.py Domain 8: pred=obs=77 identity on Tsumeb. Three decay formulas reference ghost source files.
f ∝ 1/R_Earth (stability from globe radius stability). monitor.py hardcodes pred=obs=7.83 (identity check). Dome's formulas.json contains globe spherical formula.
1-2 nT signal adopted from Tyler et al. 2003 (globe ocean tidal dynamo). M2 tide requires distant moon (two bulges). Spatial pattern requires globe ocean geography.
CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic cell positions. Axisymmetric H(r) has no φ-dependence — cannot predict longitude-specific features. 45°W threshold unfalsifiable.
Exponential decay models fitted to CHAOS-7 outputs. monitor.py Domain 8: pred=obs=77 identity. FORM-111 references nonexistent independent_verification.py.
WIN absent from all code files. -32 nT/yr has no computing script. Same prediction simultaneously CONFIRMED (WIN-042) and PENDING (PRED-012).
2.26× ratio from NOAA NP.xy in globe coordinates. monitor.py has data but never computes directional ratio. W019 contradicts (5.5× not 2.26×).
FSF = tan(lat)/tan(lat_ref) — dome parameters cancel algebraically (proven to machine precision 10⁻¹⁵). Dome's own fsf_derivation.py noticed spherical pattern.
Semidiurnal factor of 2 in M2 = T_lunar/2 is l=2 spherical harmonic (quadrupole) of tidal potential. Flat disc gives monopole spike (period = full lunar circuit). monitor.py identity check.
Same l=2 quadrupole as WIN-045. S2/M2 amplitude ratio encodes M_sun/r_sun³ vs M_moon/r_moon³. monitor.py identity check 12.0=12.0.
λ_A = c/H₀ = Hubble distance. z = D/λ_A ≡ Hubble law. H₀ = 70 from globe Cepheid ladder. Formula is tired light (Zwicky 1929), refuted by SN time dilation.
CMB anomaly defined via Y_ℓm decomposition (spherical harmonics = globe geometry). Ecliptic plane uses 23.44° obliquity.
K1 = sidereal day (23.9345h). Sidereal/solar day difference encodes orbital motion (1/T_sid = 1/T_solar + 1/T_year). monitor.py hardcodes SIDEREAL=23.9345.
O1 period encodes lunar sidereal orbital angular speed (σ = τ - s). Dome's monitor.py does NOT track O1 — likely because it requires subtracting an orbital angular speed with no flat-disc origin.
N2 encodes apsidal precession rate (Doodson p variable) — requires elliptical orbit under solar perturbation. M2−N2 beat = anomalistic month. N2/M2 ratio ≈ 3e encodes orbital eccentricity.
λ_A = Hubble distance (same as WIN-047). MOND interpolation function relabeled as ρ_A formula. Galaxy distances from globe distance ladders. FORM-050 docstring admits 'same formula as WGS84'.

Sub-H(r) depth δ=6,371 km = Earth's mean radius (no flat-disc derivation). B(r) coefficients match IGRF pole strengths. Exponential form contradicts toroidal geometry (should be 1/r). OPEN-011 unsolved.
λ_A = Hubble distance (same as WIN-047). All numbers (z, mass, velocity, 6.2σ) borrowed from globe cosmology. El Gordo at ~10²² km vs dome radius 20,015 km.
λ_A = Hubble distance. H₀ from globe Cepheid distances. Five test cases are post-hoc (published data plugged into known relationship).
Solar formula uses 23.45° (globe obliquity). No flat-disc mechanism produces latitude-dependent solar elevation matching observed pattern.
V13 coordinate system fitted to WGS84 distances. 6.2% RMSE from only 6 routes. Fails for 40% of world population (equatorial cities beyond dome's equatorial ring).
θ = −lonE follows from definition of geographic longitude. 0.9941 EW scale fitted to WGS84, no physical derivation. WGS84 dependency explicitly acknowledged (OPEN-001).
NMP deceleration from IGRF-derived positions in globe coordinates. Same IGRF dependency as WIN-006/007. 111 km/deg conversion (globe).
SAA cell position from CHAOS-7 spherical harmonics. monitor.py Domain 33 hardcoded. Axisymmetric dome field cannot predict longitude-dependent features.
Schumann resonance measurements from globe-positioned stations. G3 storm classification from NOAA Kp index (globe-calibrated magnetometer network).
1.574c = πc/2, Earth-ionosphere waveguide phase velocity. disc_diameter = 40,030 km ≈ globe circumference (40,075 km). Tesla's patent (US 787412) shows spherical Earth diagram.
Decay asymmetry ratio from CHAOS-7/INTERMAGNET data processed through spherical harmonic models.
P-wave shadow zone geometry requires spherical Earth (Snell's law in spherical coordinates, core at r=3480 km). Standard seismological explanation.
Polaris elevation predictions tested as deviations from WGS84 latitude. PRED-NEW-001 explicitly frames results vs WGS84.
NH/SH heat budget asymmetry is standard climate science (land/ocean fraction, albedo, atmospheric circulation on rotating sphere with Coriolis).
Antarctic gravity anomaly from GRACE satellite gravimetry (requires Keplerian orbits at ~500 km + WGS84 reference field for residual computation). g(r) in monitor.py = WGS84 Somigliana.

dependency_graph.description: Which globe constants flow into which dome calculations. Arrows show dependency direction.

indirectly_required_by: All 67 WINs that reference specific locations
WGS84_coordinates.note: OPEN-001 explicitly concedes this dependency remains unresolved

structural_argument.title: The dome model is a display layer over globe infrastructure
paragraphs: Of the dome's 67 claimed WINs, 61 (91%) depend on at least one globe-derived input — a WGS84 coordinate, a globe-measured constant, a spherical harmonic model output, or a formula that is spherical geometry in disguise. The remaining 6 are theological assertions (3), a literature citation (1), or qualitative claims with no numerical content (2). None of the 67 produces a prediction derived from dome physics alone.

The dependency is structural, not incidental. The dome model's fundamental parameters — disc radius (20,015 km = πR_Earth), firmament height (fitted to globe-consistent observations), sun altitude (fitted to globe-measured angles) — are all reverse-engineered from globe data. The coordinate system cannot place a single city without WGS84 (OPEN-001). The gravity formula is copied verbatim from WGS84 Somigliana (monitor.py, labeled 'source: WGS84 standard'). The eclipse predictions parasitize Besselian elements computed on a spherical Earth. The tidal periods encode Keplerian orbital mechanics the dome has no framework to derive.

The dome's own code reveals the dependency chain. distance_analysis.py optimizes ellipse parameters against distances explicitly labeled 'great circle.' find_curve.py is an empty skeleton with pass bodies. v44_pipeline.py (cited by 3 WINs) returns 404. The FSF (Firmament Scaling Function), presented as a dome-geometric quantity, is algebraically identical to tan(lat)/tan(lat_ref) — dome parameters cancel to machine precision. The 95.2% accuracy is a static HTML string with no computation.

Perhaps most telling: the dome model openly acknowledges 6 Open Problems (OPEN-001, 003, 006, 007, 011, 015) that collectively concede it cannot operate without WGS84 coordinates, has no lunar mechanics, cannot explain satellite imagery, and fails for 40% of the world's inhabited locations. These are not minor gaps — they are the load-bearing walls of any cosmological model. The dome has built a monitoring dashboard on top of globe infrastructure and called it an independent cosmology.

counter_argument_and_response.strongest_dome_defense: All scientific models bootstrap from existing data. The globe model also used earlier measurements to calibrate. Using WGS84 coordinates doesn't invalidate dome predictions — it just means the dome hasn't yet developed its own coordinate system (OPEN-001). The predictions themselves are novel regardless of the input coordinate system.
counter_argument_and_response.response: The asymmetry is fatal. When the globe model uses prior data, it derives testable predictions that differ from the inputs — Kepler used Tycho's data to predict Mars positions years ahead, which were then confirmed. The dome model takes globe outputs (IGRF field values, Besselian eclipse timing, Doodson tidal periods), relabels them with dome terminology, and calls the numerical agreement a 'confirmation.' There is no dome prediction that diverges from the globe prediction it parasitizes. If a dome-derived prediction ever disagreed with the globe value, it would fail — because the dome has no independent computation to resolve the disagreement. This is the definitional difference between an independent model and a display layer: an independent model can be wrong in its own way. The dome can only be wrong by importing a globe value incorrectly.
